transcript.text: Hello, living in Lafayette V-LOG, the 25th of April, 2013.
Today I got up at 6 o'clock, like I always do.
Got to bed about 9.30, got up at 6.
And it's about 8.49 now, so I'm going to be going to sleep here pretty soon.
Pretty much went right into Boulder because, well, what happened with...
I tried to... I wanted to make the trailer to my channel,
And Windows Movie Maker's all screwed up, dude.
My computer's a piece of shit.
Even if it's only a 10-second video, it crashes every time, and I was freaking out,
So I wanted to go down to Boulder to...
Or my mom's computer to do it with her Windows Movie Maker,
But her computer didn't work because it couldn't take the SD card.
Her jack's broken to transfer, and then her computer, Windows Movie Maker,
Doesn't take videos from my video camera like my computer.
It's weird.
Every computer has, like, a different Windows Movie Maker.
But then I have AVS Converter, and I bought that a couple years ago,
And it turns out that you can also download, I guess, for the same price.
It's the AVS Video Editor, and that's a good program,
And I was really liking that, digging on it,
Because I want to do a lot of videos in the future,
And that's the program to use, man.
It's like $30.
So I spent all day just familiarizing.
Well, first of all, going around in loops, being stupid, taking forever,
Realizing that what I needed was a different program,
And then figuring out where I'm out, and then building my trailer.
So that was pretty much my hold there.
But, you know, now I can do V-Log, make, you know, editing.
My road bike was going to be done today, too,
So it was going to be, like, killing two birds with one stone,
But then it wasn't, so it'll be done tomorrow.
So I'll have to go into Boulder tomorrow.
But there's a mountain bike I was going to fix up for my brother,
So I can do two birds with one stone again tomorrow.
So, yeah, it's warm now.
It's starting to warm up, so it's starting to come together.
Tomorrow I want to figure out my bike computer now,
And then my little GPS watch, and, yeah, and get going.
And that's that.
Ow.
Spit my tongue.
Ow.
Okay, peace out.
